title: fraxinus angustifolia var. rotundifolia
intro: Fraxinus angustifolia var. rotundifolia, also known as narrow-leaved ash, is a variety of the ash tree that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, exhibiting anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The medicinal properties of Fraxinus angustifolia var. rotundifolia have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
